2009-08-01.title: Namibians Say Inquiry on China Will Expand
2009-08-01.abstract: A shift in an investigation by Namibian prosecutors into kickbacks on government contracts signals that they suspect a pattern of corruption on deals with China.

2009-08-01.title: Iran President Denies Rift With Leader
2009-08-01.abstract: President Mahmoud Ahmadinejad defended himself amid a rising chorus of critics, while a prominent conservative gave him a stark warning.

2009-08-01.title: Musharraf Decree in ’07 Was Illegal, Court Rules
2009-08-01.abstract: Pakistan’s Supreme Court ruled that former President Pervez Musharraf’s imposition of emergency rule in 2007 was unconstitutional.

2009-08-01.title: With Boots in Iraq, Minds Drift to Afghanistan
2009-08-01.abstract: There are still some 130,000 U.S. troops in Iraq, but the war in Iraq is no longer the center of the action for the Pentagon.

2009-08-01.title: Leader Vows to Update Spain’s Economy, With Labor Rights Intact
2009-08-01.abstract: José Luis Rodríguez Zapatero, the prime minister of Spain, faces a failing economy with an 18 percent unemployment rate, the highest in the euro zone.

2009-08-02.title: Corazon Aquino Dies
2009-08-02.abstract: Corazon Aquino, who deposed an autocratic ruler and brought democracy to the Philippines, died at the age of 76.

2009-08-02.title: U.S. Pilot’s Remains Found in Iraq After 18 Years
2009-08-02.abstract: Capt. Michael Scott Speicher, a fighter pilot, was shot down in the opening hours of the first Gulf War in 1991.

2009-08-02.title: Malaysian Arrests Put in Question Vow of Rights
2009-08-02.abstract: Malaysia’s honeymoon of liberalism hit the rocks when the police broke up a large rally in Kuala Lumpur.

2009-08-02.title: Filipinos Mourn Aquino, ‘People Power’ President
2009-08-02.abstract: Thousands of Filipinos lined up in the rain for a last glimpse of former President Corazon Aquino.

2009-08-02.title: Iranian TV Report on Trials
2009-08-02.abstract: A report from Press TV, Iran's state-supported English-language broadcaster, on what critics in Iran have called "show trials."

2009-08-03.title: Arrests in Bank Robbery Create a Rift Between Iraqi Officials
2009-08-03.abstract: Three members of the presidential guard are suspected in the deadly crime, which has shocked residents of a Baghdad neighborhood.

2009-08-03.title: Hate Engulfs Christians in Pakistan
2009-08-03.abstract: The recent attacks on Christians in central Pakistan, the culmination of several days of rioting, show how precarious life is for the tiny minority there.

2009-08-03.title: Russia Accused of Altering Border
2009-08-03.abstract: Georgia said Russian reconnaissance teams entered the village of Kveshi in a disputed region in an attempt to move the boundary several hundred yards to a strategically better position.

2009-08-03.title: Genghis Khan Rules Mongolia Again, in a P.R. Campaign
2009-08-03.abstract: The 13th-century ruler has been rebranded in Mongolia on everything from an international airport to mugs, with a focus on his lighter side.

2009-08-03.title: U.S. Weighs Iran Sanctions if Talks Are Rejected
2009-08-03.abstract: Cutting off gasoline imports is being considered as an option if Iran refuses to discuss its nuclear program.

2009-08-04.title: Iraq Censorship Laws Move Ahead
2009-08-04.abstract: The government is moving to ban Web sites deemed harmful to the public, to require Internet cafes to be registered and to press publishers to censor books.

2009-08-04.title: Australia Police Hold 4 in Terror Plot
2009-08-04.abstract: Police arrested four men with suspected links to a Somalia-based Islamic organization, saying they planned to stage a suicide attack on a military base.

2009-08-04.title: Signs of Dissent Emerge in an Iranian Power Base
2009-08-04.abstract: The struggle for Iran’s future is being fought even in the remote strongholds of the country’s theocracy.

2009-08-04.title: Fears of Fraud Cast Pall Over Afghan Election
2009-08-04.abstract: With insurgents active in half the country, many Afghans remain doubtful that the election will take place.

2009-08-04.title: Newsweek Steps Up Effort to Free Reporter in Iran
2009-08-04.abstract: In advertisements, petitions and in a report on its Web site, the newsmagazine is objecting to Maziar Bahari’s treatment in the country, where he has yet to receive access to a lawyer.

2009-08-05.title: Britain: Torture Inquiry Sought
2009-08-05.abstract: A parliamentary committee called Tuesday for an independent inquiry into allegations that Britain’s security services were complicit in the torture of terrorist suspects detained after the Sept. 11 attacks.

2009-08-05.title: Bill Clinton Returns With Journalists
2009-08-05.abstract: After meeting with Kim Jong-il, the leader of North Korea, former President Bill Clinton secured a pardon for two American journalists who had been jailed there. (Video: NBC)

2009-08-05.title: Symbol of Unhealed Congo: Male Rape Victims
2009-08-05.abstract: A sudden spike in male rape cases is seen as a consequence of joint Congo-Rwanda military operations against rebels.

2009-08-05.title: Iran: U.S. Hikers Charged
2009-08-05.abstract: Iran has charged three young Americans with illegal entry into the country, Iranian state television reported Tuesday.

2009-08-05.title: Neighbors by Day, Soldiers by Night in Afghanistan
2009-08-05.abstract: An American unit is finding that its dual missions of winning friends and rooting out enemies can sometimes clash.

2009-08-06.title: After Clinton Trip, U.S. Studies Signals From N. Korea
2009-08-06.abstract: After former President Bill Clinton won the freedom of two American journalists, officials were busily calculating whether the psychology in Pyongyang had changed.

2009-08-06.title: Canada: TV Cameraman and Pilot Die in Helicopter Crash
2009-08-06.abstract: The cameraman had been recording the damage left by a tornado that hit the village of Mont Laurier, Quebec, on Tuesday afternoon.

2009-08-06.title: Russia: Military Defends Sub Patrols
2009-08-06.abstract: A senior Russian defense official dismissed concerns that patrols by two Russian submarines off the coast of the United States indicated an increase in tension.

2009-08-06.title: Britain: Release for ’88 Pan Am Bomber?
2009-08-06.abstract: Abdel Basset Ali al-Megrahi, who has been diagnosed with terminal cancer, has applied for release on compassionate grounds.

2009-08-06.title: Russia: 2nd Trial in Reporter’s Killing
2009-08-06.abstract: Lawyers for the family of Anna Politkovskaya called the new trial superfluous and asked the judge to return the case to prosecutors for further investigation, Interfax reported.

2009-08-07.title: Canada Finds Wreck of U.S. Aircraft From World War II
2009-08-07.abstract: A government agency said an underwater archaeology team had discovered an intact seaplane that failed to take off in 1942.

2009-08-07.title: Jewish Groups Say Obama’s Pick for Medal Has Anti-Israel Bias
2009-08-07.abstract: Lawmakers have also criticized the choice for the Presidential Medal of Freedom, Mary Robinson, the first woman to serve as Ireland’s president, for her work as a human rights advocate.

2009-08-07.title: C.I.A. Missile Strike May Have Killed Pakistan’s Taliban Leader, Officials Say
2009-08-07.abstract: American officials said they were growing increasingly confident that Baitullah Mehsud, the Taliban leader who has been blamed for a wave of violence across Pakistan, was dead.

2009-08-07.title: Baghdad Seeks Smoking Ban
2009-08-07.abstract: The stance is particularly aggressive — and perhaps unenforceable — in a nation where cigarettes sell for as little as 40 cents a pack and smoking is widespread.

2009-08-07.title: Scandal Puts Pressure on a Brazilian Leader to Step Down
2009-08-07.abstract: The leader of the Senate faces a nepotism and corruption scandal that threatens to hamper the president in his last year in office.

2009-08-08.title: Senator Fears Letter Sends Wrong Signal on Honduras
2009-08-08.abstract: A message from the State Department to Republicans causes anxiety for supporters of Manuel Zelaya, the ousted president of the Central American country.

2009-08-08.title: China: Tibetan Dissident Disappears
2009-08-08.abstract: A Tibetan who had written a book in support of the Tibetan uprising of 2008 and edited a banned literary magazine has disappeared, according to a Tibet advocacy group.

2009-08-08.title: Sri Lanka: Separatist Leader Arrested
2009-08-08.abstract: The leader of the remnants of the Sri Lankan separatist group known as the Tamil Tigers was arrested and taken to the Sri Lankan capital for questioning.

2009-08-08.title: Colombia President, on South America Tour, Defends U.S. Military Role
2009-08-08.abstract: President Álvaro Uribe took to the road facing skepticism among his South American neighbors over a plan to allow an increased American military presence in his nation.

2009-08-08.title: Pakistan’s No. 1 Enemy: Ex-Ally Bent by Al Qaeda
2009-08-08.abstract: The terrorist reportedly killed in a missile strike on Wednesday is perhaps the most prominent example of a Taliban fighter who turned against his country.

2009-08-09.title: China Says $100 Billion Was Bilked by Rio Tinto
2009-08-09.abstract: China said it had evidence showing that employees of the mining giant had engaged in commercial espionage for at least six years.

2009-08-09.title: Claims Differ on Pakistani Taliban Struggle
2009-08-09.abstract: Pakistani officials said a top figure in the militant group was killed in a shootout, but another Taliban commander reportedly denied the claim.

2009-08-09.title: Iraqi Officials Concerned About Threat of Swine Flu From U.S. Troops
2009-08-09.abstract: The United States said swine flu had been diagnosed in 51 American troops in Iraq, The Associated Press reported.

2009-08-09.title: Clinton Praises Angola, but Urges More Reform
2009-08-09.abstract: Secretary of State Hillary Rodham Clinton arrived in Angola to bolster ties with a country that rivals Nigeria as Africa’s biggest oil producer.

2009-08-09.title: Typhoon Pummels China and Taiwan
2009-08-09.abstract: Rain deluged China’s east coast on Monday, hours after Typhoon Morakot slammed into the region south of Shanghai, forcing one million people to flee.

2009-08-10.title: Shock Over Rampage, but Not Much Surprise About the Culprit
2009-08-10.abstract: A police officer’s shooting spree has come to symbolize a crisis in Russian law enforcement, making the issue of police criminality almost impossible to ignore.

2009-08-10.title: U.S. to Hunt Down Afghan Drug Lords Tied to Taliban
2009-08-10.abstract: U.S. commanders say the addition of drug traffickers to a “kill or capture” list is legal and a key part of a plan to disrupt the flow of drug money to the Taliban.

2009-08-10.title: Scores Dead and Hundreds Feared Missing From Typhoons
2009-08-10.abstract: The toll from two typhoons rose as a Taiwanese village was buried, leaving as many as 600 people missing, in addition to fatalities in the Philippines, Japan and China.

2009-08-10.title: Sectarian Bombings Pulverize a Village in Iraq
2009-08-10.abstract: In the most devastating attack, a pair of flatbed trucks packed with bombs exploded simultaneously in a village near Mosul.

2009-08-10.title: Iranian Lawmakers Demand Say on Cabinet, Hinting at a Rift Among Hard-Liners
2009-08-10.abstract: The lawmakers’ demands are the latest sign of a struggle that may limit the president’s political clout as he moves to form a new government in the coming week.

2009-08-11.title: N. Korean Ship Searched for Radioactive Material
2009-08-11.abstract: Authorities detained a North Korean vessel, the first time a ship has been seized and boarded under sanctions adopted by the U.N. Security Council.

2009-08-11.title: Ecuador: Area Leaders Voice Worry Over G.I.’s for Colombia
2009-08-11.abstract: Brazil and Argentina suggested that the U.S. and Colombian presidents meet with South American leaders soon to discuss a planned increase in American military forces in Colombia.

2009-08-11.title: Mexico’s Drug Traffickers Continue Trade in Prison
2009-08-11.abstract: Imprisoned drug traffickers can find a new base of operations for their criminal empires, recruit underlings, and bribe their way out for the right price.

2009-08-11.title: Nigerian Amnesty Plan Faces Difficulties
2009-08-11.abstract: Experts predict that a new arms-for-amnesty peace program was unlikely to have much impact on the militants’ sabotage campaign against the oil industry.

2009-08-11.title: Russia: Conflicting Reports of 2 Chechnya Kidnappings
2009-08-11.abstract: Two members of a nonprofit group in the Chechnya region may have been kidnapped Monday, Russian news agencies reported.

2009-08-12.title: Iraq’s Shiites Show Restraint After Attacks
2009-08-12.abstract: The Shiites, habituated to suffering by centuries as the region’s underclass, have convinced their followers they win by not responding with violence.

2009-08-12.title: Britain: $65 Million in Jewelry Stolen
2009-08-12.abstract: The heist of an exclusive jewelry store is one of the biggest diamond robberies in British history.

2009-08-12.title: Iran: French Embassy Staff Member Freed
2009-08-12.abstract: Nazak Afshar, on trial for charges related to post-election protests in Iran, has been released from the Tehran prison where she was being held.

2009-08-12.title: Somalia: 6 Hostages Are Released
2009-08-12.abstract: Four European aid workers and two Kenyan pilots had been held hostage for nine months in Somalia. The identity and aims of the kidnappers remained unclear.

2009-08-12.title: Indonesia: Terrorism Suspect Not Killed in a Shootout, Forensic Tests Confirm
2009-08-12.abstract: The man killed in a shootout on Saturday was not Noordin Muhammad Top, the most wanted terrorism suspect in Southeast Asia, Indonesian police said.

2009-08-13.title: A Turbulent Past Lurks in a Serene Swiss Lake
2009-08-13.abstract: For the orderly Swiss, munitions dropped into Alpine lakes after World War II are a kind of unsightly lump under the national carpet.

2009-08-13.title: A Window Into C.I.A.’s Embrace of Secret Jails
2009-08-13.abstract: One of the Bush administration’s most extraordinary counterterrorism programs had a fairly mundane start.

2009-08-13.title: Mexico Court Orders 22 Tied to ’97 Killings Freed
2009-08-13.abstract: Mexico’s Supreme Court found that federal prosecutors used forged evidence and false testimony to implicate 22 indigenous people in a massacre in Chiapas.

2009-08-13.title: Clinton’s Flash of Pique in Congo
2009-08-13.abstract: Secretary of State Hillary Rodham Clinton’s whole seven-nation Africa tour, which has had quite serious intentions, may end up being reduced to one sound bite about her husband.

2009-08-13.title: Envoy Sees New Role at U.N.
2009-08-13.abstract: Remarks by Susan E. Rice, the United States ambassador to the United Nations, contrasted the Obama administration’s approach with that of the Bush White House.

2009-08-14.title: A Technocrat Shakes Up the Afghan Campaign
2009-08-14.abstract: Ashraf Ghani, the most educated and Westernized of Afghanistan’s presidential candidates, is seeking to do the unexpected: defeat President Hamid Karzai.

2009-08-14.title: Where Boom Times Slow, but Never End
2009-08-14.abstract: Iron ore has brought good fortune, and a measure of economic protection, to a remote Australian town.

2009-08-14.title: China Warms to New Credo: Business First
2009-08-14.abstract: China is finding it harder to separate domestic politics from its aggressive push into the global business arena.

2009-08-14.title: Afghan Election Race Narrows
2009-08-14.abstract: The current leader, Hamid Karzai, still leads, but gains by his opponents may mean that he will be forced into a runoff.

2009-08-14.title: Corrections
2009-08-14.abstract: An article on July 10 about a bus rapid transit system in Bogotá, Colombia, that helps reduce fuel emissions erroneously included a city among poor cities in which subway service is limited, less efficient and more costly compared with the Bogotá system. Lagos, Nigeria, has no subway, though one has been proposed. A reader pointed out the error in an e-mail message several weeks after the article appeared.

2009-08-15.title: Pakistan Lifts Longtime Ban on Political Activities in Restive Tribal Areas
2009-08-15.abstract: Rights groups have long denounced the rules as draconian and Pakistan’s political parties have urged the government to do away with them, calling them a dark legacy of British colonial rule.

2009-08-15.title: Is the Mayor Fit for Office? No Sure Answer
2009-08-15.abstract: Leonid M. Chernovetsky, Kiev’s mayor, has an outsize personality, and his bizarre behavior has prompted Parliament members to say he needs a mental exam.

2009-08-15.title: South Ossetia Tries to Disarm Its Citizens
2009-08-15.abstract: After years of building tensions and, finally, a war, a people now used to carrying weapons are being asked to give them up.

2009-08-15.title: Idle Iraqi Date Farms Show Decline of Economy
2009-08-15.abstract: A nation that once had a robust farming sector is becoming dependent on imports and oil to sustain itself.

2009-08-15.title: Russia: Man Arrested in 15 Hate Killings Over 2 Years
2009-08-15.abstract: The police in Moscow have arrested a man suspected of killing 15 people because they did not look Slavic, the Interior Ministry said Friday.

2009-08-16.title: An Introduction to ‘At War’
2009-08-16.abstract: Today, we introduce you to a new blog about America at war, recognizing that war is now, and will be, a defining experience for Americans.

2009-08-16.title: Nobody Cares About Chromosomes
2009-08-16.abstract: For the first time ever, tens of thousands of military women abruptly found themselves in ground combat, mostly because warfare in Iraq and Afghanistan is all around them.

2009-08-16.title: Riccardo Cassin, 100, Dies; A Legendary Mountaineer
2009-08-16.abstract: Mr. Cassin scaled hundreds of the world’s most daunting peaks with daring, tenacity and technical brilliance.

2009-08-16.title: American Held in Myanmar Is Released
2009-08-16.abstract: An American sentenced to hard labor for intruding at the home of Myanmar’s pro-democracy leader left with a visiting American senator.

2009-08-16.title: Threats by Taliban May Sway Vote in Afghanistan
2009-08-16.abstract: The insurgents’ campaign against this week’s election may cost President Hamid Karzai vital support from his fellow Pashtuns.

2009-08-17.title: Mexico Puts New Officers on the Job at Customs
2009-08-17.abstract: The government replaced hundreds of agents at airports and border crossings in an effort to root out corruption.

2009-08-17.title: Clerics’ Call for Removal Challenges Iran Leader
2009-08-17.abstract: An anonymous letter calling Iran’s supreme leader a dictator does not pose a real threat to Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, but indicates how his status has suffered.

2009-08-17.title: Ripples of Dispute Surround Tiny Island in East Africa
2009-08-17.abstract: An island that lies along the border between Kenya and Uganda is creating a huge fuss because of the abundance there — and lack elsewhere — of fish.

2009-08-17.title: Afghanistan’s President Moves to Bolster Bid for Re-election
2009-08-17.abstract: President Hamid Karzai appeared in a live televised debate and allowed an Uzbek warlord to return to Afghanistan to rally support.

2009-08-17.title: Mubarak to Tell U.S. Israel Must Make Overture
2009-08-17.abstract: In White House meetings beginning Monday, President Obama is expected to look to President Hosni Mubarak of Egypt for help in breaking the latest Middle East deadlock, analysts said.

2009-08-18.title: A Battle as the Tide Takes Away Cancún Sand
2009-08-18.abstract: A dispute over one hotel’s attempts to replenish its dwindling beachfront is part of a continuing problem with beach erosion.

2009-08-18.title: Zimbabwe: Party Official Acquitted of Perjury Charges
2009-08-18.abstract: A statement from the Movement for Democratic Change said the charges were trumped up by elements of the old guard bent on sabotaging the new coalition government.

2009-08-18.title: Pakistan: 7 Feared Killed by Car Bomb at Gas Station
2009-08-18.abstract: The explosion, apparently detonated on a timer, occurred in Shabqadar, in the Charsadda District, north of Peshawar and near the tribal areas.

2009-08-18.title: Iran: Reformist Cleric’s Newspaper Is Shut Down
2009-08-18.abstract: Mehdi Karroubi angered Iran’s senior leaders by publicizing claims that protesters arrested after the disputed June elections were raped in prison.

2009-08-18.title: Matchmaking in India: Canine Division
2009-08-18.abstract: The mostly-male designer dogs owned by the surging middle class are having a hard time finding mates.

2009-08-19.title: U.S. Presses Pakistan on Taliban
2009-08-19.abstract: The Obama administration is concerned that Pakistan will turn its attention back to India.

2009-08-19.title: Afghan Long Shots Seek Presidency, or Another Job
2009-08-19.abstract: The 41 Afghans who have put themselves up for president are playing the game for all manner of reasons.

2009-08-19.title: In North Korea, Clinton Helped Unveil a Mystery
2009-08-19.abstract: Bill Clinton’s visit gave the Obama administration its first detailed look into a regime that looms as a major threat.

2009-08-19.title: Honduras: Military Is Accused of Abuses
2009-08-19.abstract: Amnesty International accuses the Honduran military and police of using beatings and mass arrests to punish protesters for opposing the ouster of President Manuel Zelaya.

2009-08-19.title: South Korea Launching a Rocket of Its Own Into Space
2009-08-19.abstract: North Korea said it hoped the international community would apply the same sanctions against Seoul that followed the North’s firing of a rocket in April.

2009-08-20.title: Scotland Appears Poised to Return Pan Am Bomber
2009-08-20.abstract: There were strong signs that the Scottish government had decided to release the Libyan prisoner convicted in the bombing of Pan Am flight 103 in 1988.

2009-08-20.title: A Good-News Photo Op Embarrasses 2 French Ministers
2009-08-20.abstract: The ministers’ supermarket visit was met by supportive “shoppers.” The supermarket later admitted to staging the reception.

2009-08-20.title: C.I.A. Sought Blackwater’s Help to Kill Jihadists
2009-08-20.abstract: Contractors were hired as part of a secret program to assassinate top Al Qaeda operatives, officials said.

2009-08-20.title: U.S. Officials Get a Taste of Pakistanis’ Anger at America
2009-08-20.abstract: President Obama’s representatives must defend American foreign policy initiatives in Pakistan in the face of rising anti-American sentiments.

2009-08-20.title: South Korea Agrees to Visit by North
2009-08-20.abstract: The South Korean government has agreed to allow North Korean diplomats to travel to Seoul to bring a funeral wreath for the former South Korean president Kim Dae-jung.

2009-08-21.title: U.S. and Cuba Work Together on Storms
2009-08-21.abstract: Weather is one of the few topics on which the United States and Cuban governments regularly engage.

2009-08-21.title: Witham Journal
2009-08-21.abstract: Homebridge Village was supposed to be a thriving community of senior citizens, but because of financial troubles the development has only one resident, 86-year-old Les Harrington.

2009-08-21.title: Congo’s Militias Lure Former Rebels From Burundi
2009-08-21.abstract: Since Burundi’s civil war ended in April, recruiters have been paying former combatants to fight in Congo.

2009-08-21.title: Won’t You Be His Neighbor?
2009-08-21.abstract: For two years, Les Harrington has been the only person living at a 58-unit retirement community, a casualty of a recession that has flattened Britain’s real estate market.

2009-08-21.title: Myanmar Troops Gain on Rebels as Villagers Flee
2009-08-21.abstract: For the first time in at least a decade, Myanmar controls most of its own border with Thailand, where it has been fighting ethnic Karen rebels.

2009-08-22.title: Ahmadinejad Nominee Is Wanted in ’94 Bombing
2009-08-22.abstract: Iran’s defense minister nominee is wanted in the bombing of a Jewish center in Buenos Aires, confronting Iran with another challenge to its reputation.

2009-08-22.title: Back Home, a Moroccan Rapper Sharpens His Words
2009-08-22.abstract: Muhammad Bahri, who goes by the stage name Barry, returns to his working-class neighborhood in search of material that will make his songs resonate.

2009-08-22.title: In South Korea, Freed U.S. Journalists Come Under Harsh Criticism
2009-08-22.abstract: South Korean rights advocates, bloggers and Christian pastors say the two reporters needlessly endangered North Korean refugees and the activists who help them.

2009-08-22.title: Afghan Election Poses New Tests for Washington
2009-08-22.abstract: Rather than demonstrate stability justifying an expanded U.S. commitment in Afghanistan, the Afghan presidential election underscored the challenges that remain.

2009-08-22.title: U.S. Still Using Security Firm It Broke With
2009-08-22.abstract: The State Department continues to award the company formerly known as Blackwater millions in contracts.

2009-08-23.title: Detainee to Question 9/11 Suspect
2009-08-23.abstract: A federal judge has granted a prisoner challenging his detention at Guantánamo Bay, Cuba, permission to ask questions of Khalid Shaikh Mohammed.

2009-08-23.title: Brazilian Farmers Offered Cash to Fight Deforestation
2009-08-23.abstract: Government and environmental groups are offering money in an effort to prevent farmers from cutting down rain forest.

2009-08-23.title: On Day of Mourning, 2 Koreas Meet in the South
2009-08-23.abstract: South Korea held a state funeral on Sunday for former President Kim Dae-jung, whose death brought the first major meeting with the North in nearly two years.

2009-08-23.title: Thousands Flee Athens Fires
2009-08-23.abstract: Raging fires fanned by gusting winds moved across the northern suburbs of Athens, swallowing forestland and homes.

2009-08-23.title: Barrichello Wins in Valencia
2009-08-23.abstract: After a bad pit stop on Lap 37 by the McLaren Mercedes team, its world champion driver, Lewis Hamilton, who had driven a perfect race, handed an otherwise certain victory to the man behind him, Rubens Barrichello.

2009-08-24.title: In Mexico, Ambivalence on a Drug Law
2009-08-24.abstract: Under siege by drug traffickers, Mexico took a controversial step last week when it opted to stop prosecuting those carrying small quantities of drugs.

2009-08-24.title: Accusation of Organ Theft Stokes Ire in Israel
2009-08-24.abstract: An article in a Swedish newspaper last week accused the Israeli Army of harvesting organs from Palestinians killed by soldiers, causing a diplomatic storm.

2009-08-24.title: Iraq Military Broadcasts Confession on Bombing
2009-08-24.abstract: Iraqi state channels showed brief video excerpts of a man explaining how he had organized the deadly bombing at the Foreign Ministry last week.

2009-08-24.title: Iranian Lawmakers Begin Review of Cabinet Nominees
2009-08-24.abstract: While a review of President Mahmoud Ahmadinejad’s choices will highlight divisions in the leadership, it may help restore confidence in the government.

2009-08-24.title: Dancing by the Waves, Iraqis Forget War for a Day
2009-08-24.abstract: It is amazing, but it is real: for the first time since the outbreak of the sectarian war in 2006, Iraq is enjoying a beach season.

2009-08-25.title: S. Africa Embraces Study Critical of Health Policy
2009-08-25.abstract: A decision to accept a withering assessment on AIDS and public health provided a strong signal that the country’s new leadership intends to shake up a badly managed system.

2009-08-25.title: Reformer in Iran Publishes Account of a Prison Rape
2009-08-25.abstract: While the young man was not identified, his charges were graphic and emotional, and documented official indifference — even callousness — toward his account.

2009-08-25.title: Fury Grows Over Release of Lockerbie Convict
2009-08-25.abstract: Critics say Kenny MacAskill, the Scottish justice secretary, brought shame on Scotland and jeopardized its relations with the United States.

2009-08-25.title: With Libya Ties Strained, U.S. Has Limited Options
2009-08-25.abstract: An official said the Libyan welcome of Abdel Basset Ali al-Megrahi was a setback in relations between the United States and Libya, but that it was too early to say how the administration would respond beyond angry words.

2009-08-25.title: Corrections
2009-08-25.abstract: An article on Aug. 15 about efforts by the breakaway Georgian republic of South Ossetia to disarm its residents referred incorrectly to the bullet-cartridge diameter used to identify a World War II-era Soviet machine gun owned by a woman who reminisced about her purchase of the weapon. It is 12.7 millimeters; there is no 12.7-caliber.

2009-08-26.title: Waiting for Reincarnation at a Spiritual Birthplace
2009-08-26.abstract: Some speculate that the birthplace of an especially immortalized Dalai Lama of centuries past may be where the next Dalai Lama comes from.

2009-08-26.title: Report Shows Tight C.I.A. Control on Interrogations
2009-08-26.abstract: The strong impression that emerges from newly public documents on the agency’s interrogation program is of overwhelming control from Washington, not gung-ho operatives running wild.

2009-08-26.title: Russia: 4 Police Officers Killed in Chechnya Bombing
2009-08-26.abstract: The suicide bombing was the latest in a spate of similar attacks to hit the Russian region in recent months.

2009-08-26.title: Greece: Wildfires Are Brought Under Control
2009-08-26.abstract: No injuries were reported but at least 150 homes burned down, setting off widespread anger over the state’s handling of the fires.

2009-08-26.title: Argentine Court Decriminalizes Private Marijuana Use
2009-08-26.abstract: In a unanimous ruling, the court struck down criminal penalties for possessing and consuming marijuana if it did not endanger others.

2009-08-27.title: In Front, Japan’s Opposition Senses a Chance to Govern
2009-08-27.abstract: Voter dissatisfaction — even in once-secure districts for the long dominant Liberal Democratic Party — may bring a change in Sunday’s parliamentary elections.

2009-08-27.title: A Monument to Justice, Built for Easy Escapes
2009-08-27.abstract: The ease of recent escapes from jails and courthouses has raised questions about the state of Belgium’s criminal justice system and its buildings.

2009-08-27.title: Zambia: A Reversal on Anticorruption Efforts
2009-08-27.abstract: Little more than a week after former President Frederick Chiluba was acquitted on corruption charges, Zambia effectively fired the head of its anticorruption task force.

2009-08-27.title: Russia: Concern Over North Korean Missile Testing
2009-08-27.abstract: Moscow is concerned that future North Korean tests could go awry, sending debris into Russian territory, and it is considering using surface-to-air missiles to protect itself.

2009-08-27.title: China: Rights Advocacy Aide’s Status Is Uncertain
2009-08-27.abstract: Human rights advocates and Chinese lawyers say that the assistant of a prominent rights lawyer probably was not freed by the government, as some had thought.

2009-08-28.title: Greece: Firefighting Pilot Is Killed
2009-08-28.abstract: A small water-bearing aircraft crashed while battling a wildfire on the island of Kefallonia in western Greece, killing the pilot.

2009-08-28.title: France: False ID Papers Found
2009-08-28.abstract: The police in southern France on Thursday seized a cache of false identity documents they say they think belong to the militant Basque separatist group ETA.

2009-08-28.title: Zimbabwe: A Visit From Zuma
2009-08-28.abstract: Jacob Zuma, left, began his first official visit to Zimbabwe as South Africa’s president on Thursday evening. Leaders of the Movement for Democratic Change, which joined a coalition government with President Robert Mugabe and his ZANU-PF party, say they hope that Mr. Zuma will press Mr. Mugabe to halt the arrest of its members of Parliament. Mr. Zuma’s predecessor, Thabo Mbeki, was known for his policy of “quiet diplomacy,” often criticized as appeasement of the authoritarian Mr. Mugabe, 85. Gwede Mantashe, the secretary general of Mr. Zuma’s party, said Wednesday that Mr. Zuma would “become more vocal in what we see as deviant behavior in our neighbor,” mentioning the arrest of M.D.C. lawmakers as an example of such behavior.

2009-08-28.title: Khalid bin Mahfouz, Saudi Banker, Dies at 60
2009-08-28.abstract: Sheik Mahfouz, a billionaire Saudi banker, paid $225 million to settle charges of bank fraud in 1993 and later won a string of lawsuits in Britain against writers who had accused him of supporting terrorism.

2009-08-28.title: France and Germany Warn of New Iran Sanctions
2009-08-28.abstract: Two European leaders raised the possibility of tougher measures if Iran does not show a willingness to negotiate on its nuclear program.

2009-08-29.title: A Child of the Amazon Shakes Up a Nation’s Politics
2009-08-29.abstract: The story of Marina Silva, who overcame poverty and illness to become a force in Brazilian politics, could aid her in her quest for her country’s presidency.

2009-08-29.title: Leaders Criticize Colombia Over U.S. Military Pact
2009-08-29.abstract: Some left-leaning South American presidents are condemning an accord that expands the American military presence on Colombian bases.

2009-08-29.title: U.S. Mulls Alternatives for Missile Shield
2009-08-29.abstract: Alternative plans could drop disputed sites in Poland and the Czech Republic, pleasing Russia but souring relations with allies in Eastern Europe.

2009-08-29.title: Nuclear Agency Says Iran Has Bolstered Ability to Make Fuel but Slowed Its Output
2009-08-29.abstract: The slowdown puzzled inspectors, and Iran offered no clues about whether technical problems or political considerations accounted for its action.

2009-08-29.title: Yemeni Plane’s Black Box Is Found
2009-08-29.abstract: Search teams have successfully retrieved one of the “black box” flight recorders from a Yemeni airliner that crashed in late June near the island nation of Comoros in the Indian Ocean.

2009-08-30.title: Afghanistan: An Election for Some?
2009-08-30.abstract: In some areas of Afghanistan voters took part in presidential and provincial elections, but intimidation and allegations of fraud are dogging the count.

2009-08-30.title: With Bold Stand, Japan Opposition Wins a Landslide
2009-08-30.abstract: The Democratic Party of Japan has promised to reverse the economic decline and redefine Tokyo’s relationship with the U.S.

2009-08-30.title: Voices of Afghans Who Voted, and Those Who Didn’t
2009-08-30.abstract: So different were the experiences of voters from the Taliban-influenced south and areas such as Kabul, that it was as if they were discussing elections on two different planets.

2009-08-30.title: Raikkonen Wins, Fisichella Second in Wild Belgian Grand Prix
Myanmar Army Routs Ethnic Chinese Rebels in the North
2009-08-30.abstract: The Myanmar military has overwhelmed rebels from an ethnic Chinese minority in the north of the country, the second recent victory against armed opponents of the junta.

2009-08-31.title: Panel in Iran Will Oversee Investigations Into Unrest
2009-08-31.abstract: The chief of the judiciary, who is also a rival of the president, is seeking to monitor the investigations done by presidential allies.

2009-08-31.title: Latin American Journalists Face New Opposition
2009-08-31.abstract: In Brazil, an official’s son won a court injunction against a newspaper, while a new education law in Venezuela restricts the news media there.

2009-08-31.title: German Elections Deliver Mixed Messages for Merkel
2009-08-31.abstract: Four weeks before national voting, Chancellor Angela Merkel’s party lost its majorities in two German states, but held on to power in a third.

2009-08-31.title: Growing Accounts of Fraud Cloud Afghan Election
2009-08-31.abstract: International election observers who have been working for months in Afghanistan said the problem was systemic and institutional corruption.

2009-08-31.title: Opposition Party Sweeps Japan Elections
2009-08-31.abstract: Japan’s voters cast out the Liberal Democratic Party, handing a landslide victory to the opposition Democrats in hard-fought elections.
